mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, leading to lower equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust domestic demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >50% of triggered instances over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and weakening economic momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations during tightening periods when INDPRO declines, or the regime-conditional alpha vanishes in a 2015-2018 backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 255, inflationary pressures force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when CPIAUCSL > 255, or the threshold effect is not statistically significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates a healthy labor market that supports consumer confidence and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad economic growth that boosts corporate profit expectations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters when GDPC1 growth is positive, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 100 signals a contraction in manufacturing activity that precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when INDPRO drops below 100, or the signal predictive power disappears after 2010.
